name: Aslan (Hierate / Glorious Empire)
description: The Aslan are a proud, honour-driven species who evolved from carnivore ancestors on the world of Kusyu. Aslan society is organized around clans, bound by a strict code of honour (the ihatei tradition), and driven by a biological imperative toward land ownership — the fundamental marker of Aslan status. Gender defines role as thoroughly as rank: males fight, negotiate, and hold territory; females manage finances, technology, and day-to-day clan operations. Aslan raised within the Hierate or the Glorious Empire are steeped in these traditions. The concept of face (the public display of honour) is paramount — an Aslan who is shamed must restore honour or die trying. The Glorious Empire is a separate, more aggressive Aslan state in the Trojan Reach with a reputation for expansion by force. Hierate-raised Aslan are physically identical to their Imperial cousins but their cultural drives are far stronger and their obligations to clan are non-negotiable.

traits.name: Dewclaw
traits.description: All Aslan have a dewclaw which can be extended to make for a vicious close combat weapon. The dewclaw uses the Melee (natural) skill and does 1D+2 damage.

traits.name: Heightened Senses
traits.description: Aslan have better night vision, hearing and sense of smell than humans. All Aslan receive DM+1 to any Recon and Survival checks they have to make.

traits.name: Honour Bound
traits.description: Aslan raised in the Hierate operate under a strict code of honour. They cannot back down from a direct challenge without suffering a loss of face (SOC−1 for the remainder of the encounter). Conversely, a Hierate Aslan who demonstrates superior honour or personal courage gains DM+2 on all social checks with other Aslan (Hierate or Imperial) for the remainder of that session. A deal struck with a Hierate Aslan on a matter of honour is absolute — they will not break it regardless of consequences.

traits.name: Land Sense
traits.description: The Aslan drive to acquire and hold territory is biological, not merely cultural. An Aslan who owns property (a starship of their own, a land deed, a base of operations) gains DM+1 on all Morale-equivalent checks and checks to resist fear or intimidation while on their own ground. Landless Aslan are subtly restless and may develop the ihatei drive — the compulsion to seek new territory by striking out alone.

source: Traveller Core Rulebook; Aliens of Charted Space Vol. 1, Mongoose Publishing
